name: ghpr
description: Commit, push, and open a GitHub pull request from the terminal
author: Semeton Balogun

ghpr.description: Create and push a commit, then open a GitHub pull request

options.-H, --head <branch>: Head branch (source of changes). Default: current branch
options.-b, --base <branch>: Base branch (PR target). Default: origin default branch
options.-m, --message <msg>: Commit message (required when there are changes)
options.--no-pr: Commit and push only; skip PR creation
options.--dry-run: Show what would happen without making changes
options.--json: Output results in JSON format (for programmatic use)

examples: ghpr --head feature/auth --base main -m "feat: login flow" src/auth.js

requirements.gh: Required for PR creation (unless --no-pr is used)

output.default: Human-readable output with emoji status indicators

llm-integration.detection: LLMs can detect ghpr by checking for this file or running 'ghpr --version'

recommended-flags.dry-run: Use --dry-run to validate operations before execution
recommended-flags.no-pr: Use --no-pr if you only want to commit/push without creating a PR

workflow: 1. Check if ghpr is available: ghpr --version
3. Review the JSON output to validate before proceeding
4. Run without --dry-run to execute the actual operation
